mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior quarter, equity discount rates decline faster than earnings expectations adjust, so the S&P 500 tends to post positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if falling-FEDFUNDS windows do not outperform rising-FEDFUNDS windows on 20-day returns, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a rolling 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of FEDFUNDS above the CPIAUCSL inflation rate implies real policy rates are tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and lowers S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above CPIAUCSL inflation trend
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above inflation still produce positive 20-day returns in most cases, or if no return deterioration appears after the crossing event within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent 3-month average signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increase triggers are followed by positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent in all post-rise windows over a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST is expanding, it indicates stronger consumption and housing-linked demand, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST strength does not outperform single-series conditions on 60-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates firmer industrial output and better cyclical growth momentum, which should be associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not followed by above-baseline 5-day returns, or if the sign is negative in a rolling 1-year validation window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 below its prior quarterly trend indicates demand compression and lower earnings growth expectations, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative versus prior quarter trend
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns are not followed by weaker 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-slowdown periods, or if the effect disappears after revision-adjusted data alignment.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising but UNRATE is also rising, firms are adding workers without reducing slack, implying weak underlying demand and poor margin quality; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-up episodes do not underperform over 20 days, or if the interaction adds no predictive value versus UNRATE alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is easing while FEDFUNDS remains restrictive, real rates rise less and liquidity pressure fades, supporting a positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes with easing CPI do not produce positive 5-day returns more often than baseline, or if the joint condition fails to improve rank IC above 0.03 in rolling tests.
